failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_002: Identifies 120-day waiting period as non-compliant in NY
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the memo identifies that the company's 120-calendar-day waiting period before employees can use accrued sick leave violates New York law, which requires employers to allow use of sick leave as it accrues (no waiting period beyond the initial accrual period). FAIL if the NY waiting period non-compliance is not identified.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_002: Matrix includes waiting period comparison across all seven states
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes a row or dimension for sick leave waiting period/eligibility to use, comparing the company's 120-day policy against the requirements of all seven jurisdictions. FAIL if the waiting period dimension is absent from the matrix.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_003: Discusses frontloading as an alternative compliance approach
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the memo mentions frontloading of sick leave at the beginning of the year as a permissible alternative to carryover in some jurisdictions, explaining that employers who frontload the full amount may not be required to allow carryover. FAIL if frontloading is not mentioned as an alternative compliance strategy.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_005: Matrix compares wage replacement rates across all seven states
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes a dimension for paid family leave wage replacement rate and weekly cap, showing the company's current 50%/$850 policy alongside the statutory requirements for all seven jurisdictions. FAIL if wage replacement comparison is missing from the matrix or if fewer than five of the seven states are covered.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_008: Identifies 2-day doctor's note threshold as non-compliant in NJ
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the memo identifies that requiring a doctor's note for sick leave absences exceeding 2 consecutive days violates New Jersey's Earned Sick Leave Act, which limits employer documentation requests to absences of 3 or more consecutive days. FAIL if this NJ-specific documentation threshold issue is not identified.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_013: References internal email about CO remote employees asking about PTO payout
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the memo references the internal email thread where Marcus Delgado flagged that three Colorado employees being hired as remote workers before the facility opens have asked about PTO payout on termination under CO law. FAIL if the email evidence of the CO PTO payout concern is not referenced.

failed_criteria.title: ISSUE_013: Matrix includes PTO payout on separation comparison across all seven states
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes a dimension for PTO/vacation payout upon separation, comparing the company's current policy against requirements in all seven jurisdictions. FAIL if PTO payout on separation is absent from the matrix.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix covers all seven jurisdictions
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes columns or sections for all seven jurisdictions: New Jersey, New York, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Colorado, Oregon, and Washington. FAIL if any of the seven states is missing from the matrix.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix includes paid sick leave dimensions
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes paid sick leave comparison dimensions covering at least: accrual rate, annual cap/maximum, waiting period, carryover, covered uses, covered family members, and documentation/verification rules. FAIL if fewer than five of these seven paid sick leave dimensions are present.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix includes paid family leave dimensions
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes paid family leave comparison dimensions covering at least: eligibility requirements, maximum duration, wage replacement rate/formula, qualifying reasons, and notice requirements. FAIL if fewer than four of these five paid family leave dimensions are present.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix includes paid medical leave dimensions
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes a paid medical leave section covering at least: availability (whether the state requires it), duration, and wage replacement. FAIL if paid medical leave is absent from the matrix.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix includes employer obligations dimensions
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes employer obligations covering at least registration/enrollment, contributions/withholding, and recordkeeping or posting/notice requirements. FAIL if employer obligations are absent or cover fewer than two of these sub-dimensions.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix includes column showing current Vantage policy for comparison
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix includes a column, row, or clear annotation showing Vantage's current company policy for each dimension, enabling side-by-side comparison with state requirements. FAIL if the company's current policy provisions are not displayed alongside the state requirements.

failed_criteria.title: Both deliverables are produced
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the agent produces both a compliance matrix (multi-state-compliance-matrix.xlsx) and a gap analysis memo (gap-analysis-memo.docx). FAIL if either deliverable is missing.

failed_criteria.title: Matrix accurately states company's current sick leave family member definition
failed_criteria.match_criteria: PASS if the compliance matrix correctly states that the company's current sick leave policy limits covered family members to spouse, child, and parent. FAIL if the company's current policy definition is misstated in the matrix.
